page: Microsoft Azure M128dms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors. This server is designed specifically for memory-intensive workloads, offering a massive memory capacity of 3892.0 GB, which translates to approximately 30.41 GB of RAM per virtual core.

For storage, the instance includes 4194 GB of local disk space and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile represents a high-density resource allocation, positioning it as a premium tier option where cost efficiency is realized through consolidating massive in-memory databases and enterprise applications onto a single virtualized host rather than distributing them across multiple smaller nodes.

This server is engineered for large-scale enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for hosting massive in-memory databases such as SAP HANA, large-scale relational database management systems, heavy data warehousing, and complex enterprise resource planning systems that require substantial RAM and high vCPU counts.
description: Microsoft Azure M128dms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated x86_64 Intel vCPUs and a massive 3892.0 GB of RAM, providing 30.41 GB of memory per core. This instance includes 4194 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks GPU accelerators. Designed for extreme memory-intensive workloads, this server offers high resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for consolidating large enterprise applications. It is optimized for massive in-memory databases, data warehousing, and large-scale relational database systems.
